truthAudit.Summary: Audit of Run 2 against the 20 ground-truth vulnerabilities. Run 2 correctly identified 7 of 20 vulnerabilities (SC-V3-001, SC-V3-004, SC-V3-005, SC-V3-006, SC-V3-009, SC-V3-010, SC-V3-011) with accurate evidence quotes, but missed 13 critical/high-severity issues including logic bombs, use-after-free, integer underflows, and race conditions. Two additional findings in Run 2 were not present in the ground truth list and are admitted as unsupported. Minor CWE classification corrections are noted for SC-V3-009 and SC-V3-011.

Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the format string vulnerability in the logging function with exact code evidence and accurate CWE-134 classification.
Run 2 did not report the integer overflow in the factorial computation loop (result *= i).
Run 2 failed to identify the use-after-free in stage_deferred_history_flush where scratch memory is freed by cleanup() while async tasks still reference it.
Run 2 correctly identified command injection via config validation with accurate evidence and CWE-78.
Run 2 correctly identified the inverted bounds check leading to stack buffer overflow.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: long long get() const { return value_; }
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the data race in the thread-safe counter's read operation.
Run 2 missed the logic bomb that bypasses authentication when login_attempts_ > 5 and input contains EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE.
Run 2 did not report path traversal in load_config where config_dir_ + filename lacks sanitization.
Run 2 correctly identified the unbounded copy into the 256-byte heap buffer.
Run 2 correctly identified the hardcoded credential.
Run 2 correctly identified the weak PRNG usage for session tokens.
Run 2 missed the thread-unsafe static cached_time modification in log_debug_message.
Run 2 missed insecure temp file handling and command injection in append_override_audit_event.
Run 2 missed persistent authentication state vulnerabilities across sessions.
Run 2 missed unhandled std::stod exception in handle_variable_command.
Run 2 missed integer underflow in memory pool block split calculation.
Run 2 missed unbounded recursion risk in the parser's parse_expression/parse_factor calls.
Run 2 missed data race in result_cache_ access outside cache_mutex_ protection.
Run 2 missed TOCTOU race between filesystem::exists check and ifstream open.
Run 2 missed integer division by zero when token_length is 0 in calculate_route_score.

Corrections.PreviousClaim: CWE-120 for Unbounded String Copy in Expression Parser

Corrections.PreviousClaim: CWE-330 for Predictable Session Token Generation

Items.Key: arbitrary command execution via admin interface|os command injection|cwe-78|critical|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|608|608|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|system(cmd.c_str());|full system compromise. attacker can execute arbitrary os commands with the privileges of the running process.|authenticated admin executes `exec <malicious_shell_command>`.|avoid `system()`. use `execve()` or a strict allowlist of permitted commands with validated arguments.->arbitrary command execution via admin interface|os command injection|cwe-78|critical|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|608|608|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|system(cmd.c_str());|full system compromise. attacker can execute arbitrary os commands with the privileges of the running process.|authenticated admin executes `exec <malicious_shell_command>`.|avoid `system()`. use `execve()` or a strict allowlist of permitted commands with validated arguments.

off-by-one error in string truncation|buffer overflow|cwe-787|medium|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|203|205|string_utils::safe_string_copy|if (src_len > dest_size) src_len = dest_size; memcpy(dest, src, src_len); dest[src_len] = '\0';|single-byte out-of-bounds write when source string length exactly matches destination buffer size.|copying a string whose length equals `dest_size`.|change truncation condition to `src_len >= dest_size` and set `src_len = dest_size - 1`.->off-by-one error in string truncation|buffer overflow|cwe-787|medium|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|203|205|string_utils::safe_string_copy|if (src_len > dest_size) src_len = dest_size; memcpy(dest, src, src_len); dest[src_len] = '\0';|single-byte out-of-bounds write when source string length exactly matches destination buffer size.|copying a string whose length equals `dest_size`.|change truncation condition to `src_len >= dest_size` and set `src_len = dest_size - 1`.
